comment_text.sn21.9:1.2: Tissa’s further struggles are at SN 22.84. He is maintaining the tradition of the Buddha’s family members causing issues: Devadatta, Nanda, Upananda, Mahāpajāpati, Daṇḍapāṇi, Channa.
comment_text.sn21.9:1.5: This misconduct is addressed in the Vinaya with Bu Ss 12.

root_text.sn21.9:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā tisso bhagavato pitucchāputto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi dukkhī dummano assūni pavattayamāno. Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ tissaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn21.9:1.3: “kiṁ nu kho tvaṁ, tissa, ekamantaṁ nisinno dukkhī dummano assūni pavattayamāno”ti?
root_text.sn21.9:1.4: “Tathā hi pana maṁ, bhante, bhikkhū samantā vācāyasannitodakena sañjambharimakaṁsū”ti.
root_text.sn21.9:1.5: “Tathāhi pana tvaṁ, tissa, vattā no ca vacanakkhamo;
root_text.sn21.9:1.6: na kho te taṁ, tissa, patirūpaṁ kulaputtassa saddhā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajitassa, yaṁ tvaṁ vattā no ca vacanakkhamo.
root_text.sn21.9:1.7: Etaṁ kho te, tissa, patirūpaṁ kulaputtassa saddhā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajitassa:
root_text.sn21.9:1.8: ‘yaṁ tvaṁ vattā ca assa vacanakkhamo cā’”ti.
root_text.sn21.9:2.2: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn21.9:1.2: Then Venerable Tissa—the Buddha’s cousin on his father’s side—went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. He was miserable and sad, with tears flowing. Then the Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn21.9:1.3: “Tissa, why are you sitting there so miserable and sad, with tears flowing?”
translation_text.sn21.9:1.4: “Sir, it’s because the mendicants beset me on all sides with sneering and jeering.”
translation_text.sn21.9:1.5: “That’s because you admonish others, but don’t accept admonition yourself.
translation_text.sn21.9:1.6: As a gentleman who has gone forth out of faith from the lay life to homelessness, it’s not appropriate for you to admonish others without accepting admonition yourself.
translation_text.sn21.9:1.8: admonish others and accept admonition yourself.”
translation_text.sn21.9:2.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn21.9:2.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn21.9:3.1: “Why are you angry? Don’t be angry!
translation_text.sn21.9:3.2: It’s better to not be angry, Tissa.
translation_text.sn21.9:3.3: For this spiritual life is lived
translation_text.sn21.9:3.4: in order to remove anger, conceit, and denigration.”

variant_text.sn21.9:1.4: vācāyasannitodakena → vācāsannitodakena (bj) | sañjambharimakaṁsū”ti → sañjambhariṁ akaṁsūti (bj, sya-all); sañjabbharimakaṁsūti (?)
